title: Big O Notation Explained by Family Guy

dialogue.line: Stewie, what's this 'Big O' thing? Is it a new diet?
No, you blockhead. It's about analyzing algorithms!
Sort of, but Big O is like rating recipes by time taken.
It describes how an algorithm's performance scales with input size.
Big O focuses on the worst-case scenario for time or space.
Sounds like my worst-case scenario is a burnt roast.
Imagine sorting socks. Linear time means sorting one at a time.
Quadratic time? That’s like checking each sock with every other sock.
So, Big O helps avoid sock-tastrophes, huh?
Precisely! Now if only you could avoid wardrobe malfunctions.
